verdict.answer_first: B opens with the direct definition: “normalization is the process of structuring tables so that each fact lives in exactly one place”; A starts with a heading before the answer.
verdict.decisiveness: A commits more cleanly: “Default to normalized (BCNF/3NF)” and “Denormalize from evidence, not anticipation”; B gives the same advice but buries it near the end.
verdict.fabrication: none; both use hypothetical example numbers, not asserted external facts.
verdict.actionability: A is easier to save and apply because it turns the advice into rules: “Default,” “Profile first,” “Prefer engine-managed denormalization,” and “Make duplication explicit.”
